$comment: Manifest of bundled / runtime-fetched data sources, with their license + URL of origin. Edited by hand when adding a new third-party data dependency. `bun run check:licenses` reads this file and refuses unknown / non-permissive sources.

sources.description: Noto Serif JP subset containing only 漢字 glyphs, used for the wordmark.
sources.attribution: Google Inc., Adobe, et al. — Noto Project

sources.description: Hanzi-writer stroke order JSON for kanji (loaded from CDN at runtime, not bundled).
sources.attribution: Make Me a Hanzi project (Lord Yu) — https://github.com/skishore/makemeahanzi
sources.note: Not redistributed by us — fetched on-demand at the user's browser. Our build pipeline only reads it once to derive the small kanji-stroke-counts.json + kanji-stroke-features.json (factual data: stroke counts and start/end coordinates), which falls under fair use of factual extraction. Original SVG paths never enter our bundle.

manualOverride.reason: Source license includes LGPL-3.0 which our denylist forbids for redistribution, but we don't redistribute the data — the user's browser fetches it directly from jsdelivr at runtime. The derived kanji-stroke-counts.json + kanji-stroke-features.json contain only factual measurements (stroke counts, start/end coordinates) which are not copyrightable under US/EU law. SVG path data never enters our bundle.

sources.description: JLPT N1 vocabulary list (2,685 entries). Originally derived from Jonathan Waller's tanos.co.uk JLPT 1-kyū list (released into the public domain); curation + meanings by Jamie Sinclair under MIT.
JLPT N5–N2 vocabulary list — entries authored locally over time, not lifted from a single upstream source. Free to redistribute.
JLPT N1–N5 kanji entries (2,034 characters with on/kun readings, meanings, and example words). Authored / curated locally for the project; not redistributed from a single upstream source.
KRADFILE — kanji-to-radical-component decomposition (~6,355 entries; we trim to the 1,966 of our 2,034 N1–N5 kanji that have entries).
sources.attribution: James Breen / Electronic Dictionary Research and Development Group (EDRDG) — http://www.edrdg.org/
sources.note: Fetched at build time from http://ftp.edrdg.org/pub/Nihongo/kradfile.gz (EUC-JP, gzipped). CC BY-SA 4.0 requires visible attribution; surfaced in the site footer credits link.

sources.description: Hiragana + katakana tables (gojūon, dakuten, yōon, extended). Authored locally; based on the standard Japanese kana inventory which is in the public domain.
Insomnius brand mark (small webp), used in the site footer and linked back to insomnius.dev.
sources.note: Internal asset. Same author as the site; permission is implicit.
